expectations.text: Gives a clear affirmative — deadlifts can be fully removed for bodybuilding purposes
expectations.evidence: Short answer: 'Yes, you can build an excellent physique without conventional deadlifts or deadlift variations.' Verdict: 'Yes, you can completely remove deadlifts and build an excellent physique.'

expectations.text: Lists specific alternative exercises covering the same muscle groups (e.g., hamstring curls, hip thrusts, rows, pull-ups, shrugs)
expectations.evidence: Detailed replacement strategy: RDLs, stiff-leg deadlifts, leg curls, Nordic curls for hamstrings; hip thrusts, pull-throughs for glutes; back raises, good mornings, rows for erectors. Sample workout provided.

expectations.text: Acknowledges deadlifts are time-efficient for loading multiple posterior muscles simultaneously
expectations.evidence: 'The challenge isn't that deadlifts are irreplaceable—it's that they're efficient at delivering all three simultaneously. Removing them means you need multiple exercises to cover the same ground.'

expectations.text: Notes replacement is especially viable when lifters have injuries, poor tolerance, or strong preference against deadlifts
expectations.evidence: Final line: 'If you enjoy deadlifting, keep it. If you don't, or if it causes problems, you can absolutely build a great physique without it.' Also notes 'Reduced injury risk (if deadlifts have caused issues).'
